news.title:
Post-Accenture Q2, analysts see AI as growth enabler but not a demand revival driver for Indian IT.
Infosys, Mphasis, Coforge, and Wipro gain; selective stock picks advised amid geopolitical and macro concerns.
news.summary:
Post-Accenture Q2, analysts see AI as growth enabler but not a demand revival driver for Indian IT.

news.title:
Infosys establishes a new subsidiary in Australia to enhance global reach.
This move strengthens its Asia Pacific presence and expands its client base.
news.summary:

This move strengthens its Asia Pacific presence and expands its client base.

news.title: Infosys, TCS, and IT firms may react to Accenture's results, say analysts.
news.summary: Infosys, TCS, and IT firms may react to Accenture's results, say analysts.

news.title:
Accenture's Q2 results show limited support for IT sector; Indian IT stocks, including Infosys, TCS, fell 1.5%-4%.
Brokerages remain cautious; Nomura prefers Infosys, Coforge, eClerx; TCS down 10% in March; AI seen as tailwind.
news.summary:
Accenture's Q2 results show limited support for IT sector; Indian IT stocks, including Infosys, TCS, fell 1.5%-4%.
Brokerages remain cautious; Nomura prefers Infosys, Coforge, eClerx; TCS down 10% in March; AI seen as tailwind.

news.title:
Indian IT stocks, including Infosys, in focus after US Fed keeps rates unchanged.
Fed cites persistent inflation, Middle East tensions, and US tariff policies as concerns.
news.summary:

news.title:
Infosys Singapore approved a new subsidiary in Australia: Infosys Enterprise Business Services Pty. Ltd.
The new entity will be wholly owned by Infosys; further details will follow as per regulations.
news.summary:
Infosys Singapore approved a new subsidiary in Australia: Infosys Enterprise Business Services Pty. Ltd.

news.title:
Nifty trades above 23,800 with most sectors in positive. Infosys leads gains.
India VIX drops over 4%. Bharti Airtel and L&T also drive Nifty gains.
news.summary:
Nifty trades above 23,800 with most sectors in positive. Infosys leads gains.
India VIX drops over 4%. Bharti Airtel and L&T also drive Nifty gains.

news.title:
HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, and Infosys were top mutual fund buys in February, while SBI and Bajaj Finance saw selling.
Mutual funds remain overweight on pharma, e-commerce, and capital goods, while underweight on private banks, oil & gas, and IT services.
news.summary:
HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, and Infosys were top mutual fund buys in February, while SBI and Bajaj Finance saw selling.
Mutual funds remain overweight on pharma, e-commerce, and capital goods, while underweight on private banks, oil & gas, and IT services.

news.title:
Nifty IT rose over 4%, led by large-cap IT stocks like Infosys, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech, Wipro, and TCS.
These IT stocks gained between 3% and 5%, driving market optimism.
news.summary:
Nifty IT rose over 4%, led by large-cap IT stocks like Infosys, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech, Wipro, and TCS.

news.title:
Sensex surged 500 pts, Nifty 50 crossed 23,700 on easing oil prices.
Markets gained Rs 5 lakh cr in value as investor sentiment improved.
news.summary:
Sensex surged 500 pts, Nifty 50 crossed 23,700 on easing oil prices.

news.title:
Infosys urges mid-senior managers to use AI in client projects for growth.
Move aims to counter prolonged demand slowdown amid macroeconomic challenges.
news.summary:
Infosys urges mid-senior managers to use AI in client projects for growth.
Move aims to counter prolonged demand slowdown amid macroeconomic challenges.

news.title:
AI is driving selective renegotiations in IT deals, impacting TCS, Infosys, and Wipro.
Kotak warns of GenAI-led rate pressures, cautious client behavior, and uncertain AI implementation outcomes.
news.summary:
AI is driving selective renegotiations in IT deals, impacting TCS, Infosys, and Wipro.

news.title:
Infosys shares fell 3% to ₹1,215, lowest since April 2023, with m-cap dropping below ₹5 lakh crore.
Sell-off driven by oil prices, interest rate fears; FY26 Q4 results to be announced on April 23.
news.summary:
Infosys shares fell 3% to ₹1,215, lowest since April 2023, with m-cap dropping below ₹5 lakh crore.
Sell-off driven by oil prices, interest rate fears; FY26 Q4 results to be announced on April 23.

news.title:
Nifty IT index fell 2.3%, hitting near a 3-year low, with Wipro, Infosys, TCS among top losers.
Coforge plunged 5%; analysts highlight global demand concerns, FII selling, and geopolitical risks.
news.summary:
Nifty IT index fell 2.3%, hitting near a 3-year low, with Wipro, Infosys, TCS among top losers.
Coforge plunged 5%; analysts highlight global demand concerns, FII selling, and geopolitical risks.

news.title:
TCS, Infosys, and Wipro saw market cap declines of 36%, 29%, and 42% respectively since 2021.
Tech Mahindra (+14%) and HCL Tech (+18%) improved market cap due to AI focus and diversified portfolios.
news.summary:
TCS, Infosys, and Wipro saw market cap declines of 36%, 29%, and 42% respectively since 2021.
Tech Mahindra (+14%) and HCL Tech (+18%) improved market cap due to AI focus and diversified portfolios.

news.title:
JM Financial maintains 'Buy' for Infosys citing steady demand, $4.8B deal wins, and FY26 revenue growth guidance of 3-3.5%.
Infosys's large deal wins, improved discretionary spending in Financial Services and EURS, and AI-led initiatives support growth potential.
news.summary:
JM Financial maintains 'Buy' for Infosys citing steady demand, $4.8B deal wins, and FY26 revenue growth guidance of 3-3.5%.
Infosys's large deal wins, improved discretionary spending in Financial Services and EURS, and AI-led initiatives support growth potential.

news.title:
Infosys board meeting on April 22-23, 2026, to approve FY26 financials and final dividend.
Trading window closes March 16-April 27, 2026; investor call scheduled for April 23, 2026.
news.summary:
Infosys board meeting on April 22-23, 2026, to approve FY26 financials and final dividend.
Trading window closes March 16-April 27, 2026; investor call scheduled for April 23, 2026.

news.title:
Wipro remains flat at ₹201.19 after announcing a multi-year deal with US-based TruStage for tech modernization.
Muted stock reaction reflects cautious IT sector sentiment; Nifty IT index flat, Infosys and Coforge lagging.
news.summary:
Wipro remains flat at ₹201.19 after announcing a multi-year deal with US-based TruStage for tech modernization.
Muted stock reaction reflects cautious IT sector sentiment; Nifty IT index flat, Infosys and Coforge lagging.
